stories.title: Burnham on track to be next PM as 322 Labour MPs back him
stories.summary: Nominations have opened in the Labour leadership contest to replace Keir Starmer, with Andy Burnham the only declared candidate and already backed by 322 Labour MPs. Burnham is widely expected to become the next Prime Minister unopposed.

stories.title: Louise Haigh accuses 'cabal of men' around Keir Starmer of mistreating women in cabinet
stories.summary: Former Transport Secretary Louise Haigh has said she was one of several female cabinet ministers who were victims of 'sexist and unpleasant' press briefings from a group of senior men around Starmer. The accusations add to questions about the culture within No. 10 during Starmer's tenure.

stories.title: Burnham will make good PM, Starmer says as nominations open
stories.summary: Sir Keir Starmer has publicly endorsed Andy Burnham as his successor, saying Burnham 'will make a good prime minister' as Labour's formal leadership nomination process gets underway.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs, debates, legislation

stories.title: Iran Urgent Question debated in House of Commons
stories.summary: An urgent question was tabled in the House of Commons on the renewed conflict between the US and Iran, brought by Conservative MP Dame Priti Patel. The session highlighted cross-party concern about UK foreign policy and the regional escalation.

stories.title: Farage launches preemptive attack on Commons standards investigation
stories.summary: Nigel Farage has launched a preemptive attack on the parliamentary standards process ahead of an investigation into his undisclosed £5m gift, accusing the Commissioner of political bias. The investigation was paused when Farage resigned his seat to trigger the Clacton by-election.

stories.title: Coming up in the Commons 6–10 July
stories.summary: Parliament's schedule for the week includes Westminster Hall debates on e-petitions and the Sir David Amess summer adjournment debate on 16 July. The final sitting days before recess are focused on outstanding legislation.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy, internal politics

stories.title: Andy Burnham apologises for Labour's stance on Gaza and says it 'didn't get it right'
stories.summary: PM-in-waiting Andy Burnham has apologised for Labour's approach to the Gaza war, saying the party must 'do better' and that he will put more pressure on Israel. The move is seen as an attempt to win back Muslim and progressive voters lost during Starmer's tenure.

stories.title: Labour MPs to rebel on party funding reforms and demand total ban
stories.summary: Labour MPs are threatening to rebel over political funding reform legislation, demanding tougher measures including a full ban on certain foreign-linked donations. The rebellion is partly fuelled by ongoing controversies around Nigel Farage and Reform UK's finances.

stories.title: Burnham's Gaza apology marks 'reset moment' for Labour
stories.summary: Analysts suggest Burnham's public apology over Gaza represents a significant 'reset moment' for Labour as it seeks to rebuild its fractured electoral coalition. Questions remain over how much will substantively change in policy terms.

stories.title: Badenoch purges would-be MPs who back net zero
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch has rejected former Conservative MPs as parliamentary candidates for the next general election because they support net zero climate targets. The move signals a sharp rightward shift on environmental policy under Badenoch's leadership.

stories.title: Badenoch: Conservatives won't stand in Clacton by-election
stories.summary: Kemi Badenoch told BBC Breakfast that the Conservative Party will not stand a candidate in the Clacton by-election triggered by Nigel Farage's resignation, saying 'we don't march to the beat of Nigel Farage's drum.'

topics.name: Liberal Democrats
topics.description: Liberal Democrat party news and policy positions

stories.title: Ed Davey labels Burnham 'Avanti Andy' and warns of rail chaos
stories.summary: Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey has branded Andy Burnham 'Avanti Andy' during a visit to Torbay, Devon, warning that Burnham's devolution proposals could push the government 'even further' from fixing the railway chaos affecting passengers.

stories.title: Lib Dems push for ban on MPs taking money from X, citing MAGA threat
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats are pushing for legislation to ban MPs from receiving payments or donations from Elon Musk's X platform, citing concerns about foreign influence and the MAGA movement's reach into British politics.

topics.name: Reform UK
topics.description: Reform UK and Nigel Farage news

stories.title: Police investigate £500,000 Reform UK donations from mother of fraudster
stories.summary: Police are investigating donations to Reform UK from Fiona Cottrell, mother of convicted fraudster George Cottrell — a close associate of Nigel Farage. The probe is examining potential evasion of restrictions on political donations.

stories.title: Five pressing questions for Reform UK about its finances
stories.summary: Amid a parliamentary standards investigation into an undisclosed £5m gift to Farage, The Guardian sets out five key questions about the opaque financial operations of Reform UK, including questions about donor vetting and internal controls.

stories.title: George Cottrell referred to Farage as 'daddy', says ex-Reform candidate
stories.summary: New revelations about convicted fraudster George Cottrell's close ties to Nigel Farage have emerged, with a former Reform candidate saying Cottrell was introduced as Farage's 'chief of staff'. Reform insists Cottrell held no official role.

topics.name: SNP
topics.description: Scottish National Party and Scottish politics

stories.title: SNP wins Holyrood 2026 but fails to secure majority
stories.summary: John Swinney led the SNP to victory at the 2026 Scottish Parliament election but fell short of the majority he needed. Labour and Reform UK tied for second place in a historically significant result for Scottish politics.

stories.title: SNP's McAllan backs more 'evidence-led' North Sea drilling
stories.summary: SNP Energy Minister Màiri McAllan has signalled support for more evidence-based offshore fossil fuel development, softening the party's previous 2023 stance against new North Sea licensing.

stories.title: Cabinet Office publishes List of Ministers' Interests for July 2026
stories.summary: The Cabinet Office has released the updated List of Ministers' Interests for July 2026, detailing public appointments and financial interests declared by ministers in line with the Ministerial Code.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results and polls

stories.title: Farage's Clacton by-election officially confirmed for 13 August
stories.summary: Reform UK MP Lee Anderson moved the writ in the House of Commons to officially trigger a by-election in Clacton, with a likely polling date of 13 August. Nigel Farage resigned his seat to stand again after facing a parliamentary standards investigation.

stories.title: Reform activists urged to switch focus from Manchester to Clacton
stories.summary: Reform UK activists who had been preparing to campaign in the Greater Manchester mayoral by-election have been urged to divert resources south to Clacton for Farage's contest, raising questions about the party's bandwidth.

stories.title: Reform by-election campaign risks a replay of the Johnson error
stories.summary: Commentators warn that by triggering an optional by-election, Nigel Farage has handed opponents weeks to publicly revisit his financial controversies — drawing comparisons to Boris Johnson's ill-fated Uxbridge gamble.
